item.title: Banned horror title Horses begins recouping development costs after selling 30,000 copies
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 16, 2026

2 Min Read

Santa Ragione's surreal horror title Horses, which was banned on Steam and the Epic Games Store, has begun recouping its development costs after selling 30,000 copies worldwide and generating $100,000 in revenue.

In an email sent to Game Developer, Santa Ragione co-founder and studio director Pietro Righi Riva explained the Italian company doesn't have enough cash to kickstart production on another project but has managed to successfully pay back its private investors.

"For anyone curious about where that went: of the $100k, $30k went to the game's director, Andrea Lucco Borlera, who spent years working on it in his own time, and $62k went to the game's private funders, covering their $50k investment and providing a return," said Riva. "That leaves $8k for Santa Ragione. It isn't enough to begin anything new, but it's a start toward recouping our $50k in development costs."

Santa Ragione previously said it was facing closure after Horses was banned from Steam over content platform owner Valve described as concerning. After Epic also decided to block Horses from launching on the Epic Game Store, the title eventually made its debut on Itch.io, GOG, and the Humble Store. 

Related:UK social media ban will restrict 'high-risk' features on game platforms

Horses went on to win multiple awards including the 2026 IGF Nuovo Award and Best Experience at the Italian Video Game Awards.

Riva said he is "eternally grateful" to everybody who supported the studio over the years and hopes the difficulties surrounding the distribution of Horses sparked important conversations around censorship.

"Horses uses grotesque, subversive imagery to confront power, faith, and violence. We reject subjective obscenity standards and believe this kind of moralizing censorship evokes a darker past in which vague notions of 'decency' were used to silence artists. Games are an artistic medium and lawful works for adults should remain accessible," said Santa Ragione in a statement when Horses was banned on Steam. 

"We respect players enough to present the game as intended and to let adults choose what to play; lawful works should not be made unreachable by a monopolistic storefront’s opaque decisions."

The final update for Horses was rolled out this week and brings Japanese, Korean, Spanish, German, French, and Russian language support. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/horses-begins-recouping-development-costs-after-selling-30-000-copies

item.title: Ubisoft Ends Assassin’s Creed Shadows Support With Black Tides, the True Finale Hunting Naoe and Yasuke at Last
item.content:encoded:
Today, Ubisoft is releasing the final content drop update for Assassin's Creed Shadows. Patch 1.1.11, which varies in size from the 9GB of the Mac version to the 26.1GB of the Xbox version, introduces new story content, a major endgame feature, live crossover events, and a wide range of bug fixes.

First, there's a new quest called Black Tides, which serves as the true concluding chapter for protagonists Naoe and Yasuke. Two elite Templars known as the Black Cross arrive in Japan to hunt the protagonists for the chaos they've caused, though both agents may be pursuing their own hidden agendas. To unlock the quest, players must have completed the main stories of both protagonists, as well as the two previous post-launch story drops (A Critical Encounter, which introduced rogue Rufino, and A Puzzlement, which added Kane and Kinu). Notably, having finished the Claws of Awaji expansion is not required. For players who skipped the post-launch content entirely, this effectively means there is now a three-chapter story arc waiting before this final quest, making it the de facto "real" ending of the game.

Then there's Domains, the headline new feature for hardcore players, accessible at level 30. Created in-world by MOD, an Assassin hacker, as a set of combat and survival trials for Dark Animus users, it consists of 5 gameplay simulation arenas across 10 escalating difficulty levels, with the challenge designed to force players to rethink their builds: a strategy that works in one Domain may fail entirely in the next. The feature comes with its own dedicated merchant, in-game currency, and progression tree where players can purchase Animus exploits, new endgame gear sets, powerful engravings, and fan-favorite legacy weapons. Crucially, none of these rewards will ever appear in the store or the exchange, as they are exclusively earned through Domains and intended as pure bragging-rights items for the most dedicated players.

A third Animus Rift called Horizon is also now available, continuing the Eagle/Guide storyline. Players must have completed both previous Animus Rifts to access it.

Several Assassin's Creed Shadows combat adjustments are included in Patch 1.1.11, too:

  • Damage boost for Shurikens fired from the Bank Shot and Triple Threat mastery upgrades
  • Armor-destroying abilities are now half as effective against bosses
  • Enemies now take fall damage from shorter heights, increasing overall fall damage
  • Damage from enemy Poison Kunai and Explosive Grenades is reduced
  • Up to 6 Corrupted Castles are now available each season

A batch of adrenaline-related perks have been rebalanced to percentage-based values rather than fixed ones, including Adrenaline on Headshot, Adrenaline on Critical Hit, and several others. Key item buffs include:

  • Whisper of Gold Long Katana perk now grants 100% ability damage (up from 50%)
  • Ethereal Cloth Headgear perk now triggers at below 15% health (up from 10%)
  • Pink Petal Kosode and Sakura Bloom Armor now convert adrenaline to 20% health on death (up from 10%)
  • Teppo and Bow weapons can now be engraved with armor damage and armor-piercing perks

Finally, the Animus HUB is now accessed exclusively through the Shadows Memory page in the system menu, harmonizing in-menu and in-gameplay navigation. The Forge engraving list has been reordered with a new Critical Category added, and world map pins and markers now correctly persist through travel between Japan and Awaji. On that note, the patch also adds two new crossover Projects in the Animus HUB, Riptides and Undertow, which unlock themed outfits, weapons, trinkets, and other rewards. Players activate them by completing Anomalies and progressing through the reward tracks, just like previous HUB projects. These rewards span both Assassin’s Creed Shadows and the upcoming Black Flag Resynced, so progress can be made by playing Anomalies in either game. One important limitation is that the crossover Projects will not be available on Nintendo Switch 2, since the game isn't coming to the console.

Did you enjoy your time with Assassin's Creed Shadows? Let us know by leaving a comment below.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/assassins-creed-shadows-black-tides-finale/

item.title: Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival launches October 8
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Survival horror action game dated.

Survival horror action game Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam on October 8, Saber Interactive and Boss Team Games announced.

The game will be available in the following editions:

  • Standard Edition ($39.99) – physical or digital
  • Deluxe Edition ($49.99)
    • Full details on the Deluxe Edition’s contents will be revealed closer to launch.

Pre-orders for any edition of the game will unlock the exclusive in-game Labyrinth Pistol Skin and Martyr Sawblade Skin at release.

Here is an overview of the game, via Saber Interactive:

About

Embark on a battle for survival against Hell’s armies with a terrifying new story in the Hellraiser universe in Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival! Descend into the darkest depths of the underworld to rescue your love from the depraved clutches of the Cenobites. Face your deepest fears against deviant wretches, cultists and creatures with the infernal powers of the Genesis Configuration puzzle box and an arsenal of earthly weapons.

Key Features

  • A New Nightmare in the Hellraiser Universe – Discover a chilling new story in the iconic horror franchise as you unwittingly kickstart Pinhead’s (voiced by Doug Bradley) ritual to unleash the ultimate torment on the world. We assure you: the pain will be exquisite.
  • Wield the Might of Hell – Harness the unique powers of the Genesis Configuration, including pyrokinesis, telekinesis, and the iconic Hell Chains, to inflict bone-breaking, flesh-searing pain (or pleasure?) upon your enemies. The Genesis Configuration is also instrumental in solving puzzles and transforming parts of the winding Labyrinth.
  • Battle for Survival Against Hell’s Armies – Take on Hell’s wretches, deviants, cultists, and its most infernal priests in an unholy war for your immortal soul, all while trying to thwart the sinister plans of the Cenobites.

Watch the trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/clive-barkers-hellraiser-revival-launches-october-8
